do_not_copy: Do not copy source visuals, layouts, screenshots, brand marks, event assets, staging imagery, or proprietary presentation materials.
observed_move: Reserve a peak slide for the strongest outcome so the story has a visible moment of arrival.
why_it_works: Large-scale keynote pacing needs a crest. If every slide has the same intensity, the audience cannot feel the product argument land.
ppt_translation: Create an editable big-number, chart, or dominant object climax that compares generated deck quality states, breaks the prior grid, and makes the largest object encode the outcome.
quality_risk: The climax is weak if it repeats an earlier layout, uses the same density, or lacks a clear before-and-after proof object.

extraction_units.source_anchor: large-scale keynote pacing pattern: outcome crest
extraction_units.derived_rule: Give the strongest justified measured result the largest visual field in the deck.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not make all comparison arms equal size on the outcome slide.
extraction_units.qa_probe: The strongest supported delta is visually largest and traceable to the score table.

extraction_units.source_anchor: large-scale keynote pacing pattern: visible arrival moment
extraction_units.derived_rule: Break the prior proof-grid rhythm so the audience feels a moment of arrival.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not reuse the same card grid or status strip from proof slides.
extraction_units.qa_probe: The climax slide has a distinct composition from the preceding proof slide.
